scores.note: The film clearly finds the load-bearing argument: a July crisis widened because it landed inside an already primed European system, rather than touring the article's full catalog evenly.
It now walks the non-automatic-alliance objection and the agency-blur failure mode, but the hardest boundary still stays somewhat safer and more abstract than the notes themselves demand.

The proof beats finally tie the anti-single-cause thesis to the July chain and separate stronger evidence from weaker deep causes, though they still compress part of the notes' evidentiary chain into smooth conclusion.

Time is the right quantity and it is used to reason, but the exact 25/7/5-day counts are not actually surfaced in the survey notes, so the film's quantitative authority outruns its stated ground truth.
It names the rejected single-culprit alternative and costs the chosen systemic account honestly: cleaner blame is lost unless Germany's larger share stays in frame.
The recap now does rule: persuasive on escalation, weak on ranking deep causes, residual risk is blame-washing, and the Germany caveat is the thing to watch.

critiques.problem: "The quantity the notes actually let us reason with is time. There are twenty-five days ... It is seven days ... Then only five more days ..." is a useful argument, but those exact counts are not present in `analysis/wwi-causes.md`, which the film says is its ground truth.
critiques.fix: Either add the exact date arithmetic to the survey notes with the same confidence the film uses on screen, or recut the numbers scene around quantities the notes already surfaced explicitly and then reason from those.

critiques.problem: "The page is thinner on London's choice than on Vienna, Berlin, and St. Petersburg" is not a claim the notes establish, and the beat still softens the hardest edge case into commentary instead of a ruling.
critiques.fix: Ground this objection in the notes' actual boundary: Britain retained policy freedom and the Entente was not automatic, so alignments explain pressure rather than compulsion. Then say explicitly whether the article still holds, degrades, or breaks at that boundary.

critiques.problem: "The blank cheque, Russian mobilization, and the westward war plan create more than one live route" is much better than pure slogan, but it still smooths over one of the notes' named mechanism steps: France's reaffirmation of Russia as the crisis widened.
critiques.fix: Make the proof chain visibly note-backed all the way through: Vienna's casus belli reading, Berlin's blank cheque, France reaffirming Russia, Russia's failed partial mobilization, and German westward planning. Then state that this is why overdetermination is earned as an escalation account rather than as a ranking of deep causes.

summary: This draft is no longer a tour of every subsection; it has a real spine, a real trade-off, and a verdict that finally adjudicates. But it still misses pass because one of its main proof instruments, the numbers scene, steps outside the survey notes, and one hard boundary case still leans on unsupported meta-commentary instead of a note-grounded ruling.
